title: Couratari fagifolia
taxonomy: Couratari fagifolia is a species of plant that belongs to the family Lecythidaceae. This family includes other plants such as the Brazil nut tree. Couratari fagifolia is a member of the genus Couratari, which consists of various tropical trees. The plant is native to Central and South America, specifically in the tropical regions of countries like Mexico, Costa Rica, and Panama. It is also known as the couratari tree or the couratari nut tree.
uses: Couratari fagifolia has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in South America, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bark and leaves are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
